NEW RULE OF TAXATION COVERS


ASSOCIATED PRESS FRANCHISE

pages.raw_text:
assessment than is In vogue In any
other western state. Inasmuch as
the word rate" is used differently
in different western states and
much confusion has arisen In the
minds of the newspaper readers az
to the meaning of the word, the
real work of the convention should
be stated in different terms. Take
the railroads of the first class, for
instance, the trunk lines of the
Northern Pacific and Great North
ern. According to the figures,
these properties as to track and
rolling stock will under the new
rule be assessed at s17,6SS per mile.
This means that the total railse
will aggregate somewhere near
$13,000,000 in the entire state. At
an average tax rate of 35 mills,
this means about $3.50.


Comparing results under the
scheme now to be put into action
1t may be said that the present
rate in this county is S10,0S2 per
mile for trackage and rolling stock.
In 1902 the figure was only 1890.
From 1902 the results of the assess
ment under the new rule the ad-
vance OF trackage and rolling stock
of these first class roads has been
increased from 5000 to $17.6ss. a
difference of s966S. or an increase
of over 100 per cent In four years.


The above are the principal
points of public interest In the
transactions of the past week at
Olympia.
